title: OBO Credential
description: Pre-transaction artefact carried by an agent declaring its authority to act on behalf of a principal. Defined in draft-obo-agentic-evidence-envelope-00 §3.

obo_credential_id.description: Stable, globally unique identifier for this credential.

principal_id.description: Identifier of the principal on whose behalf the agent acts. Format is scheme-specific (DID, URN, or domain-qualified identifier).

agent_id.description: Identifier of the agent. MUST be bound to the credential by signature.

operator_id.description: Identifier of the entity operating the agent. MAY equal principal_id for self-operated agents.

binding_proof_ref.description: Reference to the proof of binding between principal and agent (e.g. signed delegation record, consent receipt, legal instrument).

intent_namespace.description: The governed intent namespace within which this credential is valid.

action_classes.description: The action classes this credential authorises. A=read-only, B=reversible write, C=irreversible write, D=systemic.

governance_framework_ref.description: Stable pointer to the machine-readable governance framework (ontology pack digest, policy snapshot ref, or equivalent).

expires_at.description: Unix epoch seconds at expiry. MUST be present. Short-lived credentials are RECOMMENDED.

issuer_id.description: Identifier of the credential issuer. The domain component is used to resolve the _obo-key._domainkey DNS TXT record.

credential_digest.description: SHA-256 digest of the canonical JSON serialisation of this credential (excluding this field). Format: sha256:<hex>.

scope_constraints.description: Optional additional scope constraints beyond action class.

why_ref.description: Upstream rationale authority reference for regulated lanes.

offline_verifiable.description: When true, the credential MUST be verifiable without network access.

corridor_binding.description: Identifier of the corridor this credential is bound to, when corridor-specific issuance applies.
